summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated client can write to internal __oxia/-prefixed keys through the public gRPC Write RPC. Both __oxia/commit-offset and __oxia/last-version-id were overwritten with attacker-controlled values.
expected_security_impact: Writing to __oxia/commit-offset and __oxia/last-version-id should succeed without error, demonstrating that the internal key namespace has no write protection on the public API.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction built the Oxia server from source, started it in standalone mode, and sent Write RPCs for __oxia/commit-offset and __oxia/last-version-id. Both writes returned Status_OK, confirming the internal keys were overwritten with attacker-controlled values.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts the Oxia standalone server, sends Write RPCs for __oxia/commit-offset and __oxia/last-version-id via a Go gRPC client, and checks that both writes return Status_OK. Success is signaled by the exploit exiting with code 0.
evidence_produced_by_run: Write to __oxia/commit-offset returned Status_OK (no validation error)
Write to __oxia/last-version-id returned Status_OK (no validation error)
Both internal metadata keys were overwritten by an unauthenticated client without any error

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
